_note: The OPERATOR-FACE posture overlay for the tool face (GAP 3 door-width). SEPARATE registry from remote_exposure.json BY DESIGN: remote_exposure.json governs the CONNECTOR (untrusted REMOTE client — tight); THIS governs the OPERATOR FACE (Tim, trusted, local — wider). Two threat models, two doors; never widen the connector to widen the operator. The bridge gate reads operator_posture from here (wins over remote_posture) so a tool the operator MAY run that the connector can't is declared here. DEGRADE-CLEAN: absent file or absent row → the remote posture alone governs (so the door stays fail-closed, never auto-leaks).
_door_width_ruling: lead 2026-06-19. The fix for the 37 deny-by-default tools is COMPLETE THE CLASSIFICATION, not default-allow (keep the fail-closed floor — no auto-leak). PHASE 1 (NOW, this file): widen READS only — pure no-side-effect tools get operator_posture=safe → run free. Consequential WRITES (run_role/create/capture/save_cascade/ingest/dials/context-compact) are NOT widened yet — they wait for the unified-floor attribution (#1b operator-token) to go live, then PHASE 2 adds them (+ locked + hazard-confirm) so a write inherits the floor, never an ungated fire-path. PHASE 2 = the propagation pass, classified deliberately (the coverage mechanism drafts, lead judges the consequential ones). Truly-dangerous verbs (explicitly_denied in remote_exposure.json: node/connect/set_config/run_graph/cc_clone/cc_gate/session_post/resolve_surfaced/approve/dispatch/checkpoint/revert/mark/channel_act/self_change_log) NEVER ride this door — dedicated gated routes only.
